creator: madpilot@FreeBSD.org
text:
(In reply to Dries Michiels from comment #15)

I'm sorry but I really don't understand what you're talking about in this comment, what exactly did you stumble on?

As I said in previous comments, it is not clear to me what should be done.

I don't know exactly how the calibre server works, never used it.

I personally don;t have a use case for it.

If you clearly describe your use case, maybe something can be done.

I will not modify how the calibre server code works, but I can modify the RC script if a clear thorough description of what is expected is given. Please don't ask me to cobble one up myself from multiple posts, it is not going to work because, as I said, I don't use the calibre server and have no use for it myself, so from my vintage point it is all quite cloudy. [*]

The only use I see is customizing the rc script to run as my user serving my library in my home directory, for my own devices. (which I do not need though, moving files around with command line is quite enough for my needs)

To sum it up, I have no vision of what exactly you are asking for, please describe it to me without assumption on how I use calibre and maybe I'll understand.


[*] I don't mean to say it is a useless functionality, I simply say I'm unable to see a use for me, but I could be terribly wrong, please show me light then!
